body.title: pe_chain truncates ticket description at 600/400 chars — cuts off structured fields
body.result: pe_chain.py: 4 description slice sites (pe_plan 857+875, pe_situate 1063, pe_hypothesize 1669) replaced with _DESC_CAP_REASONING=4000 and _DESC_CAP_FALLBACK=2000. Module-level docstring explains the motivation. 5 regression-guard tests cover constants, source-usage, and sub/over-cap slicing behavior. Commit (soon-push). Igor needs restart to pick up.
body.description:
pe_chain pre-reasoning steps cut the ticket description before sending it to the tier.2 model:
- pe_plan line 857: description[:600]
- pe_plan line 875 (fallback): description[:200]
- pe_situate line 1063: description[:600]
- pe_hypothesize line 1669: description[:400]

The /ticket skill template requires structured sections (Affected files / Design rules / Scope boundary / Test plan) that land near the end of the description. For today's T-consult-confidence-threshold-raise (710 chars), the 600-char cap severed the Test plan field. Evidence: qwen returned a 40-char truncated plan ('Update `CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD` to .') — the value that belongs after 'to' is elsewhere in the description AND the format hints qwen needed (e.g. 'Affected files:' cue) were cut.

Fix: raise caps to a generous bound — 4000 for reasoning-prompt contexts (PLAN/SITUATE/HYPOTHESIZE) and 2000 for the fallback-plan path. Tickets generally stay <2000 chars with the template; 4000 gives 2x headroom without prompt bloat.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/tools/pe_chain.py (4 slice sites), tests/test_pe_chain_basic.py or new tests/test_pe_chain_description_cap.py.
**Design rules:** test-plan-or-why-not.
**Scope boundary:** IN — raise description caps in pe_plan/pe_situate/pe_hypothesize. OUT — dynamic token-budget shaping, per-tier caps, truncation of other basket fields.
**Test plan:** regression-guard tests that assert the prompt carries the full description when it's ≤4000 chars, and a synthetic 5000-char description is truncated to 4000 (not 600/400).
